title: Light Up
intro: Light Up is a logic puzzle played on a grid of black and white cells.
goal: Your goal is to place light bulbs on white cells so that all white cells are illuminated.
board_example: An example board is shown below.
rule_illuminate: When placed on a square, a light bulb illuminates all cells in its row and column until the light is blocked by a black cell.
rule_numbered_cells: A numbered black cell indicates exactly the number of bulbs that must be placed next to it (horizontally or vertically adjacent).
rule_numbered_cells_detail: Black cells without numbers place no restriction on adjacent bulbs.
example_numbered: In the example below, the two bulbs placed satisfy the numbers "2", "2" and "1" next to them:
rule_no_touch: No two bulbs may be placed such that one is within the illumination path of another, unless there is a black square between them blocking their light.
example_invalid: The example below shows an invalid placement (first column) and a valid placement (green) of two bulbs sharing a column.
solved: The puzzle is solved when all white cells are lit, no two bulbs illuminate each other, and all numbered black cells have the correct number of adjacent bulbs.
solved_board: Below is a fully solved board:
control_cycle: Left click to cycle forward, right click to cycle backward between:
control_place_bulb: Placing a lightbulb
control_mark_cross: Marking the cell with an (to indicate no lightbulb will go there)

compact.intro: Place light-bulbs in white cells so that:
compact.rule_no_touch: No two bulbs () illuminate each other.
compact.rule_numbered: Any black cell with a number (0–4) must have exactly that many bulbs adjacent orthogonally.
